message:
perf(view,rpc): optimize interactive rendering and RPC bash output truncation

Interactive view rendering (src/interactive/view.rs):
- clamp_to_terminal_height: replace two-pass approach (count all
  newlines, then find cut position) with single-pass memchr jump
  that stops as soon as the (max_newlines+1)-th newline is found,
  reducing work from O(2n) to O(n) with early exit
- render_conversation: replace Vec<&str> line collection (~80KB alloc
  for 10K-line conversations) with memchr newline counting for total
  lines, then use .lines().skip(start).take(count) iterator to emit
  visible lines without intermediate allocation — eliminates per-frame
  heap allocation for the conversation viewport
- render_messages: push_str the rendered message before moving it into
  the cache (was cloning the String, which can be several KB for tool
  output), saving one allocation per uncached message render

RPC bash output truncation (src/rpc.rs):
- Pre-check whether truncation is needed using memchr line count before
  writing the full output to a temp file, avoiding the temp file write
  entirely when output fits within limits
- Write full output to temp file before calling truncate_tail() so the
  file is written from the original String rather than the truncated
  result
- Pass owned String to truncate_tail() (API now takes owned String
  to enable zero-copy fast path when no truncation is needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
